signals.headline: 76ers defeat Celtics in Game 2 with dominant second-half run** | YES (series shift confirmed) | HIGH confidence
signals.raw:
76ers defeat Celtics in Game 2 with dominant second-half run** | YES (series shift confirmed) | HIGH confidence

The 76ers capped off an impressive Game 2 victory in Boston with a 20-8 run, indicating a significant momentum shift in their first-round matchup against the Celtics.[2] This result suggests the series is more competitive than initial expectations.

signals.headline: Kevin Durant confirmed to return for Game 2 vs
signals.raw:
Kevin Durant confirmed to return for Game 2 vs. Lakers** | YES (injury update confirmed) | HIGH confidence

Kevin Durant is set to return for the Rockets in Game 2 against the Lakers, removing a major uncertainty that had affected Houston's title viability.[5] This substantially improves the Rockets' competitive position in their matchup against Los Angeles.

signals.headline: Wembanyama suffers concussion in Spurs-Trail Blazers Game 2** | YES (injury confirmed) | HIGH confidence
signals.raw:
Wembanyama suffers concussion in Spurs-Trail Blazers Game 2** | YES (injury confirmed) | HIGH confidence

San Antonio's MVP finalist Victor Wembanyama exited Game 2 against Portland after a hard fall and was ruled out, representing a significant development for the Spurs' playoff prospects.[5]

signals.headline: Thunder advance with dominant Game 1 victory** | YES (series lead confirmed) | HIGH confidence
signals.raw:
Thunder advance with dominant Game 1 victory** | YES (series lead confirmed) | HIGH confidence

Oklahoma City defeated the Grizzlies 117-115 in Game 1, establishing early control in their first-round series.[3]

The most significant market-moving development is Durant's confirmed return, which directly impacts Lakers-Rockets series odds by restoring Houston's offensive capability.
